members.name: Drive
members.summary: Distortion intensity. Controls how much of the input audio is affected by the distortion curve by moving from a linear function to a nonlinear one. Value can range from 0 to 1.

members.name: KeepHfHz
members.summary: High-pass filter, in Hz. Frequencies higher than this value will not be affected by the distortion. Value can range from 1 to 20000.

members.name: Mode
members.summary: Distortion type. Changes the nonlinear function used to distort the waveform. See [enum Mode].

members.name: PostGain
members.summary: Gain after the effect, in dB. Value can range from -80 to 24.

members.name: PreGain
members.summary: Gain before the effect, in dB. Value can range from -60 to 60.

values.name: MODE_ATAN
values.summary: Flattens the waveform in a smooth manner, following an arctangent curve. The audio decreases in volume, before flattening peaks to [code]PI * 4.0[/code] (linear value), if it was normalized beforehand.

values.name: MODE_CLIP
values.summary: Flattens the waveform at 0 dB in a sharp manner. [member drive] increases amplitude of samples exponentially. This mode functions as a hard clipper if [member drive] is set to 0, and is the only mode that clips audio signals at 0 dB.

values.name: MODE_LOFI
values.summary: Decreases audio bit depth to achieve a low-resolution audio signal, going from 16-bit to 2-bit. Can be used to emulate the sound of early digital audio devices.

values.name: MODE_OVERDRIVE
values.summary: Emulates the warm distortion produced by a field effect transistor, which is commonly used in solid-state musical instrument amplifiers. [member drive] has no effect in this mode.

values.name: MODE_WAVESHAPE
values.summary: Flattens the waveform in a smooth manner, until it reaches a sharp peak at [code]drive = 1[/code], following a generic absolute sigmoid function.

constants.name: MODE_CLIP
constants.summary: Flattens the waveform at 0 dB in a sharp manner. [member drive] increases amplitude of samples exponentially. This mode functions as a hard clipper if [member drive] is set to 0, and is the only mode that clips audio signals at 0 dB.
